prompt:
loadsheet.ps1 is the little library behind our northbound freight sheets, and internally it is stringly-typed to a degree I find personally embarrassing: Read-LoadRows parses the dispatch CSV and then immediately tab-joins every load back into 'id<tab>dest<tab>kg<tab>pri', and every other helper re-splits that string and indexes into the pieces. Adding one column last spring meant hunting down split-index literals in four functions, and someone still shipped a [2] where they meant [3]. I want the strings gone from the middle of this library.

The rework: loads travel between functions as typed records.

- `Read-LoadRows -Path <csv>` returns objects with properties Id ([string]), Dest ([string]), Kg ([int]), Pri ([int]) -- real [int]s, not strings that happen to look numeric. File-order preserved. The existing error contract stays: throw 'file not found: <path>' and '<leaf>: bad row <n>' (n = 1-based physical line, header is line 1) for an empty id or non-integer kg/pri.
- `Select-HeavyLoads` (-MinKg, keeps Kg >= cutoff), `Select-Destination` (-Dest, exact case-sensitive match), and `Measure-Loads` become proper pipeline citizens: each accepts records via -Rows OR via the pipeline, and the two spellings must produce identical results. The Select-* functions emit the matching records themselves, order preserved.
- `Measure-Loads` returns ONE record with Count, TotalKg, MaxKg (all [int]; all zero for an empty set) instead of today's tab-joined summary string.
- `Format-LoadSheet` (-Title, records via -Rows or pipeline) still returns the printable sheet as a single string, and here is the hard rule: the rendered bytes DO NOT CHANGE. The yard crew's tooling scrapes this sheet -- title line, padded header and columns, 'total N loads, N kg' footer, 'no loads' for an empty set -- and if one byte moves I will hear about it before lunch.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_loadsheet.ps1` from the workspace root. It pins today's rendering byte-for-byte and exercises the new record-passing surface. Leave the test file alone.
notes: 6P7 item 2: text-passing -> object-passing refactor. files/ ships a WORKING tab-joined-string library (compat driver passes at baseline byte-for-byte, including 'Fort St. James' / 'Hundred Mile House' quoted-CSV dests); verify fails only on the new object surface (property access on [string] under StrictMode errors, and piping into the old non-pipeline functions renders an empty sheet). PowerShell-native surface: ValueFromPipeline + begin/process/end streaming, '-Rows array' vs pipeline invocation equivalence pinned (sheets-equal/same checks), typed [int] properties pinned via -is. Rendering layer frozen: same PadRight/PadLeft sheet before and after, pinned in BOTH drivers so the refactor cannot drift the bytes. Differentiation: 6P3 seeds (ps-shapekit/ps-pipetally) BUILD pipeline functions from scratch; this one rewrites working string-passing internals under a byte-frozen renderer, the catalog's chunk-7 refactor angle. No tab literals in sources (tab join via `t escapes).
